verdicts.evidence: All three module-scope side effects described in the finding are unchanged: the four singletons are still constructed at bare module scope (was 1297-1300, now 1329-1332), and the safe-mode tool-section gate still runs at module scope, only its env read changed from parse_bool_env("XPOOL_SAFE_MODE") to get_settings().xpool_safe_mode. _install_nodriver_cookie_compat still monkey-patches nodriver's cdp_network.Cookie.from_json in place guarded only by a marker attribute (body is byte-identical apart from Dict->dict); it is called from app_lifespan in both baseline and HEAD, so only the two bare-module-scope instances (singletons, safe-mode gate) carry the 'side effect of import' defect, unchanged.

verdicts.new_quote:
async def _resolve_profile_selection(
    user_data_dir: str | None,
    *,
    force_clone: bool = False,
    source_override: Path | None = None,
    source_kind: str | None = None,
    clone_suffix: str | None = None,
) -> dict[str, Any]:
    master = _master_profile_dir()
    clone_root = _clone_root_dir()
    snapshot = _master_snapshot_dir()
verdicts.evidence: Read the full function body (now 1087-1193, was ~1080-1160): the same 4-way source_override/snapshot.exists()/master.exists()/else-raise branch, the same ~14 collaborator calls (_master_profile_dir, _clone_root_dir, _master_snapshot_dir, _is_relative_to, _profile_has_running_browser, _next_available_explicit_dir, _snapshot_needs_refresh, _refresh_master_snapshot_if_safe, _clone_profile_dir_for_session, _unique_clone_dir, _available_clone_dir, _spawn_background_sweep, _protect_clone_dir, _copy_clone_from_source), and the same loosely-typed dict[str, Any] role coordination with sibling _fallback_profile_selection (line 1196) are all present verbatim. Only mechanical ruff/pyupgrade typing changes occurred (Optional[X] -> X | None, Dict -> dict); no god-file split or decomposition happened, matching the stated expectation that none of the Stage-3 fix plans executed.

_with_cdp_timeout is unchanged in substance: it still only wraps the coroutine in asyncio.wait_for and raises a client-facing Exception on timeout, with no call to Runtime.terminateExecution or any other command to actually stop browser-side execution (only asyncio.TimeoutError -> bare TimeoutError is a mechanical Python 3.11 change). Confirmed cdp_function_executor.py:818 still has its own separate 'asyncio.wait_for(self.inject_and_execute_script(tab, js_code), timeout=10.0)', and terminateExecution is still only referenced once in the whole package, at cdp_function_executor.py:143 inside the list_cdp_commands catalog, never invoked automatically on any timeout path.

verdicts.new_quote:
if file_path:
        save_path = Path(file_path)
        save_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
        await _with_cdp_timeout(tab.save_screenshot(save_path), instance_id=instance_id)
        return f"Screenshot saved. AI agents should use the Read tool to view this image: {save_path.absolute()!s}"
verdicts.evidence: take_screenshot's explicit-file_path branch is unchanged: it writes to whatever path the caller supplies with zero validation against the shared per-user output root, while its own no-file_path branch (line ~2130) falls back to response_handler.clone_dir for oversized screenshots -- the same three-implementations disagreement the finding describes is still present verbatim (only str(...) -> !s formatting changed).

verdicts.current_location: src/stealth_chrome_devtools_mcp/embedded/server.py (all former os.getenv sites, e.g. :211-233, :51-62, :199, :4222); centralizing schema at src/stealth_chrome_devtools_mcp/settings.py:38-80
verdicts.evidence: Grepped server.py for os.getenv(/os.environ and got zero matches -- every previously ad-hoc getenv call (12+ sites) now reads get_settings().<field>. Read settings.py:1-80: it is a genuine single pydantic-settings BaseSettings model with typed fields, Field(validation_alias=...) for legacy unprefixed names, extra="forbid" to reject unknown keys, and an lru_cache'd get_settings() -- exactly the 'central config schema/single source of truth' the finding said was missing. This is a real architectural fix, not a relocation: the module's own docstring states it replaces scattered ad-hoc env-parsing helpers.

verdicts.evidence: inject_and_execute_script, call_javascript_function, execute_python_in_browser and execute_cdp_command have docstrings byte-identical to the original quote -- the naming/purpose ambiguity across this cluster is unchanged. execute_script's docstring did gain a large new async-safety warning block (sync XHR, blocking loops, alert/confirm/prompt, upload_file redirection) not present at baseline, but that addition is about runtime safety, not about differentiating it from inject_and_execute_script -- both still read as paraphrases ('Execute JavaScript...' vs 'Inject and execute custom JavaScript code') with no naming taxonomy distinguishing the six 'run code in the page' tools.

...

verdicts.evidence: Read all four functions verbatim; every docstring quoted in the original finding is unchanged, including clone_element_complete's own docstring using 'extracts ALL element data' to describe a 'clone_'-named function, clone_element_to_file still delegating to file_based_element_cloner.clone_element_complete_to_file while extract_complete_element_to_file delegates to the differently-named file_based_element_cloner.extract_complete_element_to_file, and extract_complete_element_cdp's 'most accurate' ranking still living only in docstring prose, not the name. No renaming or consolidation occurred.

The docstring's Returns contract is still just 'Optional[Dict[str, Any]]: Complete state information' with no mention of partial or detail_error, while both the TimeoutError branch and the generic Exception branch still return {'partial': True, 'detail_error': ...} degraded records (and an even thinner 'state: unknown' variant when the instance isn't in the fallback list). Only the timeout source (get_settings().browser_state_timeout_seconds instead of a raw env read) and except asyncio.TimeoutError -> except TimeoutError changed; the doc/contract mismatch itself is untouched.
